description:
You are an automation specialist refining a workflow.  
    A Git repository already exists at  
    /home/user/demo_repo  

    Your objective:  
    1. While inside that repository, produce a plain-text file that lists every file currently tracked by Git.  
    2. Save this list (and nothing else) to /home/user/git_files.log  

    Verification requirements (very strict):  
    • The command you run must query Git for the list of tracked files; do NOT hard-code the names.  
    • /home/user/git_files.log must contain one path per line, with no leading or trailing whitespace.  
    • The paths must appear in the exact order returned by the Git command you choose (the automated test will compare byte-for-byte).  
    • Ensure a final newline character is present after the last line.  

    Summary of what should exist when you are done:  
    • /home/user/git_files.log – a UTF-8 text file containing the repository’s tracked file paths, one per line.  
      (The automated test will cat this file and compare it against the expected output.)  

    Note: Only one concise terminal command is needed to meet all requirements.
truth:
Pre-existing repository location: /home/user/demo_repo

    Tracked file list (and the exact expected contents of /home/user/git_files.log):
    README.md
    scripts/run.sh
    src/main.py

    The file must end with a trailing newline (i.e., there is an invisible \n after 'src/main.py').

    Permissions: /home/user is writable by the agent; no elevated privileges are required.
name: task_000000_beff73f4
